prompt.stem: The admission charge to a cinema is different for adults and children. Admission for 2 adults and 3 children costs £20. Admission for 4 adults and 4 children costs £34. What does admission cost for 6 adults and 2 children?
The $n$th term of a sequence is $2n - 5$. Which row in the table is correct for this sequence?

A fair spinner has eight equal sections. Each section has one number written on it, as shown. The spinner is spun twice, and the two numbers scored are added. What is the probability that the sum of the two numbers is 5?

PQRS is a square with side length $x$. M is the midpoint of side PS. A circular arc, with centre M, is drawn inside the square from S to P. Another circular arc, with centre P, is drawn inside the square from S to Q. What is the area of the shaded region in terms of $x$?

A balloon contains $5000\text{ cm}^3$ of gas. The gas in the balloon gradually escapes so that the volume of the balloon decreases. 60% of the volume of the balloon is lost each week. What is the volume of the balloon, in $\text{cm}^3$, after 3 weeks?

Consider the four lines with the following equations.
$$1:\quad 2x + 6y = 3 \qquad 2:\quad 9y = 3x - 4 \qquad 3:\quad 2y = 6x + 3 \qquad 4:\quad 4x + 6y - 9 = 0$$
Which two lines are perpendicular?
The equilateral triangle PQR has sides of length 8 cm. A circle, centre O, passes through each of the vertices of the triangle. Find an expression for the circumference of the circle, in cm.

Find the sum of the solutions of
$$2\left(\frac{x}{4} + 3\right)^2 - \left(\frac{x}{4} + 3\right) - 36 = 0$$
When the expression $(2x + 3)^2 - (x - 3)^2$ is written in the form $p(x + q)^2 + r$, where $p$, $q$ and $r$ are constants, what is the value of $r$?
Which one of the following expressions is equivalent to $\dfrac{a(ab - bc)}{c}$?

The table shows statistics relating to the test marks of two groups of students.

| | group X | group Y |
|---|---|---|
| number of students | 10 | 20 |
| mean | 36 | 48 |
| range | 16 | 21 |

The results for the two groups are combined. What can be deduced about the mean and range of the combined results?

The number of pairs of winter boots sold on a day is inversely proportional to the cube of the outside temperature on that day, measured in °C. On a day when the outside temperature is 8°C, 250 pairs of boots are sold. The next day, when the outside temperature is $x$°C, the number of pairs of boots sold is 700% more than on the previous day. What is the value of $x$?

In a sale, all prices are reduced by 25%. A customer calculates the pre-sale price of a bicycle incorrectly by increasing the marked sale price by 25%. The customer's calculated pre-sale price is incorrect by £15. What is the correct pre-sale price of the bicycle?

A paint colour is a mixture of red paint, blue paint and yellow paint. The ratio of red paint to blue paint in the mixture is $18:5$. The ratio of blue paint to yellow paint in the mixture is $p:3$. The ratio of red paint to yellow paint in the mixture is $12:5$. What is the value of $p$?

In the diagram, QS is perpendicular to PR. $PS = x$ cm, $PQ = y$ cm, $QR = z$ cm, $\angle QRS = 61^\circ$. PSR is a straight line. [diagram not to scale]

Which one of the following is an expression for the length $z$, in cm?

Two identical fair six-sided dice each have their faces numbered from 1 to 6. Both dice are thrown, and the number on each of the dice is recorded. They are then both thrown again, and the number on each of the dice is recorded. What is the probability that at least one of the four recorded numbers is even?
The quadratic equation $2x^2 - px - 4 = 0$, where $p$ is a positive constant, has two solutions that differ by 6. What is the value of $p$?

Two vertices of a square are at $(1, 1)$ and $(3, 5)$. What is the difference between the perimeters of the largest and smallest possible squares that can be drawn with these points as two of their vertices?

The point M is $(2, 5)$ and the point N is $(-3, -1)$. MN is rotated $90^\circ$ clockwise about the origin to give RS. RS is then translated by the vector $\begin{pmatrix}p\\q\end{pmatrix}$ to give TU. The coordinates of the midpoint of TU are $(7, -2.5)$. Find the vector $\begin{pmatrix}p\\q\end{pmatrix}$.

A solid cone has a base radius $x$ cm. The ratio of the perpendicular height of the cone to the radius is $5:2$. A solid hemisphere of radius $y$ cm is made from the same material. Which one of the following is a correct expression for $\dfrac{\text{volume of the cone}}{\text{volume of the hemisphere}}$?

(Volume of a cone $= \frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$; Volume of a sphere $= \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$)

The diagram represents the structure of a charged atom (ion) of one isotope of an element. Which diagram represents the structure of an oppositely charged ion of a different isotope of the same element?

A soldering iron has a copper tip of mass 2.0 g. The tip is heated with 30 W of thermal power. In 50 s, the temperature of the tip increases by 200°C. How much energy is transferred from the tip to the surroundings in this time?

(specific heat capacity of copper $= 400\text{ J kg}^{-1}\text{ }^{\circ}\text{C}^{-1}$)

A water wave travels in a shallow tank from region X into region Y. In region X the angle between the wave peaks and the boundary is $\alpha$. In region Y the angle is $\beta$. What are the angle of incidence and the angle of refraction, and in which region is the speed of the wave greater?

options.label: A
options.text: incidence: $\alpha$; refraction: $\beta$; speed greater in X

options.label: B
options.text: incidence: $\alpha$; refraction: $\beta$; speed greater in Y

options.label: C
options.text: incidence: $\alpha$; refraction: $90^\circ-\beta$; speed greater in X

options.label: D
options.text: incidence: $\alpha$; refraction: $90^\circ-\beta$; speed greater in Y

options.label: E
options.text: incidence: $90^\circ-\alpha$; refraction: $\beta$; speed greater in X

options.label: F
options.text: incidence: $90^\circ-\alpha$; refraction: $\beta$; speed greater in Y

options.label: G
options.text: incidence: $90^\circ-\alpha$; refraction: $90^\circ-\beta$; speed greater in X

options.label: H
options.text: incidence: $90^\circ-\alpha$; refraction: $90^\circ-\beta$; speed greater in Y

validation.answer_text: incidence: $\alpha$; refraction: $\beta$; speed greater in X

prompt.stem: A sample of an ideal gas is sealed in a cylindrical container by a piston. The particles move with average speed $v$ and collide with the piston at frequency $f$. The piston is slowly pushed in until the gas occupies half its original volume at the same temperature. What is the new average speed and collision frequency?

Liquid nitrogen is stored in a vessel with double walls (poor thermal conductor), a vacuum gap, a shiny inner surface and a shiny outer surface. Which of the following statements explain(s) why these features help to insulate the liquid nitrogen?

1. The shiny inner surface of the inner wall is a good emitter of thermal radiation.
2. Thermal radiation cannot travel in a vacuum.
3. The shiny outer surface of the outer wall is a poor absorber of radiation.

Uranium-238 ($^{238}_{92}$U) decays by a series of alpha and beta$^-$ emissions to become lead-206 ($^{206}_{82}$Pb). How many beta$^-$ particles are emitted in the decay of one uranium-238 nucleus to lead-206?

A dc electricity transmission system: the first country supplies electricity at 400 kV and 2000 A; the second country receives electricity at 160 kV and 4000 A. What is the percentage efficiency of the system and how much energy is wasted every minute?

The primary coil of an ideal transformer is connected to 240 V mains. A lamp L on the secondary has 12 V across it. An identical lamp and switch S are also connected to the secondary. With switch open, primary current is 0.10 A. The switch is closed. What is the current in the primary coil and in lamp L?

Two fixed rails 12 cm apart carry a freely moveable rod of length 20 cm at 90° to the rails. A uniform magnetic field of 0.50 T is directed perpendicularly into the page. The rod has weight 0.40 N. The switch is closed, giving 2.4 A in the circuit. What is the initial acceleration of the rod and in which direction?

(gravitational field strength $= 10\text{ N kg}^{-1}$)

A circuit contains a 12 V battery, a thermistor and a fixed resistor in series. The graph shows how the resistance of the thermistor varies with temperature. When the temperature of the thermistor is 10°C the current is 25 mA. What is the current when the temperature is 80°C?

Two trolleys move towards each other. One trolley has mass 8.0 kg travelling right at $4.0\text{ m s}^{-1}$; the other has mass 2.0 kg travelling left at $1.0\text{ m s}^{-1}$. They collide and stick together. How much kinetic energy is transferred to other forms of energy?

Visible light travels through a vacuum at $300\,000\text{ km s}^{-1}$ with wavelengths 400 nm to 750 nm. A source emits electromagnetic waves at frequencies between $6.0\times10^{12}\text{ Hz}$ and $6.0\times10^{14}\text{ Hz}$. Which statement is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Infrared waves are emitted, but not ultraviolet or visible light waves.

options.label: B
options.text: Infrared and visible light waves are emitted, but not ultraviolet waves.

options.label: C
options.text: Infrared, ultraviolet and visible light waves are all emitted.

options.label: D
options.text: Ultraviolet waves are emitted, but not infrared or visible light waves.

options.label: E
options.text: Ultraviolet and visible light waves are emitted, but not infrared waves.

validation.answer_text: Infrared and visible light waves are emitted, but not ultraviolet waves.

prompt.stem:
Cubes of side 2.0 cm are tightly packed into a box with internal dimensions $12.0\text{ cm}\times10.0\text{ cm}\times6.0\text{ cm}$. Each cube is either solid concrete or solid steel. There are twice as many steel cubes as concrete cubes. What is the total mass of the cubes in the box?

(density of concrete $= 2.0\text{ g cm}^{-3}$; density of steel $= 8.0\text{ g cm}^{-3}$)

A car of mass 800 kg travels in a straight line along a horizontal road. The car accelerates non-uniformly from rest for 5.0 seconds and then moves at constant speed, as shown in the distance-time graph. What is the average resultant force acting on the car over the time for which it is accelerating?

A radioactive isotope has a half-life of 120 minutes and decays in a single step to a stable isotope. The initial count rate is 910 cpm. After 240 minutes the count rate is 238 cpm. After a further 360 minutes, a third measurement is made. What is the background count rate and the expected third measurement?

P and Q are two fixed points on the surface of the ocean 6.0 m apart. An ocean wave travels from P to Q at frequency 0.50 Hz and constant speed. A wave peak passes Q at $t = 0$ s. The next wave peak passes P at $t = 0.80$ s. What is the speed of the wave?

A parachutist of mass 80.0 kg drops from a plane travelling at $40.0\text{ m s}^{-1}$, 2000 m above the Earth's surface. The parachutist hits the ground at a speed of $5.00\text{ m s}^{-1}$. How much work is done by the parachutist against drag forces during the fall?

(gravitational field strength $= 10.0\text{ N kg}^{-1}$)

A solid uniform sphere of metal density $\rho_S$, radius $r$, volume $V$ falls through a viscous liquid of density $\rho_L$. Drag force $D = krv$ where $v$ is speed and $k$ is a constant. What is the terminal speed?

(gravitational field strength $= g$)
A rocket in space burns its fuel at a constant rate, applying a constant force to the rocket. What is happening to the magnitude of the acceleration of the rocket?

options.label: A
options.text: It is increasing at an increasing rate.

options.label: B
options.text: It is increasing at a constant rate.

options.label: C
options.text: It is increasing at a decreasing rate.

options.label: E
options.text: It is decreasing at an increasing rate.

options.label: F
options.text: It is decreasing at a constant rate.

options.label: G
options.text: It is decreasing at a decreasing rate.

validation.answer_text: It is increasing at an increasing rate.

prompt.stem:
A light spring of unstretched length 0.10 m has spring constant $20\text{ N m}^{-1}$. A load of mass 0.050 kg is attached. The load is pulled until the spring length is 0.30 m, then released. What is the speed of the load when the spring returns to its unstretched length?

(gravitational field strength $= 10\text{ N kg}^{-1}$; resistive forces negligible)

Use the following data table to answer the question.

| gas | melting point / °C | boiling point / °C |
|---|---|---|
| hydrogen | -259 | -253 |
| nitrogen | -210 | -196 |
| oxygen | -219 | -183 |
| neon | -249 | -246 |
| argon | -189 | -186 |

Water and carbon dioxide were removed from a sample of air and the remaining mixture was cooled to -260°C. The three most abundant remaining elements are to be separated by fractional distillation. In which order would these three elements be collected?

Consider only the first three metals in Group 1 (Li, Na, K) and only the first three elements in Group 17 (F, Cl, Br). Which of the following statements is/are correct for the compound lithium bromide?

1. It is formed from the least reactive of the three Group 17 elements.
2. It is formed from the least reactive of the three Group 1 elements and the Group 17 element (of the three) with the lowest boiling point.
3. It is formed from the Group 1 element (of the three) with the highest melting point.

Which of the following tests could be used, on its own, to distinguish between all three of the following white solids: potassium carbonate, calcium chloride and sodium sulfate?

1. Add a small amount of each solid separately to a platinum wire and hold in a colourless flame.
2. Dissolve a small amount of each solid separately in deionised water and add a few drops of sodium hydroxide solution.
3. Dissolve a small amount of each solid separately in deionised water and add a few drops of hydrochloric acid, followed by barium chloride solution.

Molecule J is a straight-chain hydrocarbon containing one carbon-carbon double bond. The relative atomic mass ($A_r$) of hydrogen is 1 and carbon is 12. What is the minimum additional information that is needed in order to determine the molecular formula of molecule J?

1. The percentage by mass of carbon in the molecule.
2. The percentage by mass of hydrogen in the molecule.
3. The relative molar mass ($M_r$) of the molecule.

Iodic acid, $\text{HIO}_3$, can be made from iodine in the following reaction:
$$\text{I}_2 + w\text{H}_2\text{O} + x\text{Cl}_2 \rightarrow y\text{HIO}_3 + z\text{HCl}$$
What is the value of $x$ when the equation is balanced?
Which one of the following formulae is correct for the compound given?

Which of the following statements about elements in the Periodic Table is/are correct?

1. When the element in Period 5, Group 2 reacts with the element that is in Period 3, Group 17, a redox reaction occurs.
2. In each Group, the elements from Period 2 are more reactive than the elements from Period 5.
3. The compound formed between the element in Period 2, Group 14 and the element in Period 3, Group 17 will have a simple molecular structure.

Some dilute aqueous solutions were electrolysed using graphite electrodes. Which of the rows in the table show(s) the correct products of electrolysis?

| Row | Aqueous electrolyte | Cathode product | Anode product |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | potassium hydroxide | potassium | oxygen |
| 2 | copper(II) chloride | chlorine | copper |
| 3 | sodium sulfate | hydrogen | sulfur |

A paper chromatogram is set up with an orange food colouring spotted on the baseline. Ten minutes after the start, the solvent front has moved 15.0 cm from the baseline and a yellow spot is 12.0 cm above the baseline. Five minutes later, the solvent front has moved up a further 10.0 cm. How far from the baseline will the yellow spot be 15 minutes after the start?

Which of the following equations represent(s) a redox reaction?

1. $\text{K}_2\text{Cr}_2\text{O}_7 + 2\text{KOH} \rightarrow 2\text{K}_2\text{CrO}_4 + \text{H}_2\text{O}$
2. $8\text{HNO}_3 + 3\text{C}_2\text{H}_6\text{O} + \text{K}_2\text{Cr}_2\text{O}_7 \rightarrow 2\text{KNO}_3 + 3\text{C}_2\text{H}_4\text{O} + 7\text{H}_2\text{O} + 2\text{Cr(NO}_3)_3$
3. $\text{H}_2\text{O} + \text{SO}_2 \rightarrow \text{H}_2\text{SO}_3$

$100\text{ cm}^3$ of ethane is mixed with $1400\text{ cm}^3$ of oxygen and ignited. All volumes measured at 150°C and atmospheric pressure. What will be the total volume of gas after complete combustion?

(Assume equal amounts of any gas at the same temperature and pressure occupy the same volume.)

10 g of a mixture of solid $\text{Mg(OH)}_2$ and solid NaOH is added to excess water. One component dissolves; the other is completely insoluble and is filtered off. $50\text{ cm}^3$ of $1.0\text{ mol dm}^{-3}$ sulfuric acid exactly neutralises the remaining solution. What is the mass of magnesium hydroxide in the original mixture?

($M_r$: $\text{Mg(OH)}_2 = 58$; NaOH $= 40$)

Calcium carbonate reacts with hydrochloric acid:
$$\text{CaCO}_3(s) + 2\text{HCl}(aq) \rightarrow \text{CaCl}_2(aq) + \text{H}_2\text{O}(l) + \text{CO}_2(g)$$
Line P shows the $\text{CO}_2$ volume vs time when 4.0 g of $\text{CaCO}_3$ reacts with $50\text{ cm}^3$ of $1.0\text{ mol dm}^{-3}$ HCl at 20°C. A second reaction used the same mass of $\text{CaCO}_3$ but $50\text{ cm}^3$ of $2.0\text{ mol dm}^{-3}$ HCl. Which line (A-F) best represents the second reaction?

($M_r$: $\text{CaCO}_3 = 100$)

Phosphoric(V) acid, $\text{H}_3\text{PO}_4$, ionises in water:
$$\text{H}_3\text{PO}_4 \rightleftharpoons \text{H}^+ + \text{H}_2\text{PO}_4^-$$
$$\text{H}_2\text{PO}_4^- \rightleftharpoons \text{H}^+ + \text{HPO}_4^{2-}$$
$$\text{HPO}_4^{2-} \rightleftharpoons \text{H}^+ + \text{PO}_4^{3-}$$
$0.1\text{ mol dm}^{-3}$ hydrochloric acid has pH 1.0 at room temperature. Which of the following statements is/are correct?

1. The pH of $0.1\text{ mol dm}^{-3}$ sulfuric acid is greater than 1.0 at room temperature.
2. $\text{H}_2\text{PO}_4^-$ can act as an acid or as a base.
3. 30 cm$^3$ of calcium hydroxide solution exactly neutralises 20 cm$^3$ phosphoric(V) acid solution when both are at the same concentration.

Complete combustion of 1 mol of hydrocarbon X requires exactly 8.5 mol of oxygen. Incomplete combustion of 1 mol of hydrocarbon X, to form carbon monoxide and water only, requires exactly 5.5 mol of oxygen. How many hydrogen atoms are there in one molecule of hydrocarbon X?

An element Z forms an ionic compound $\text{ZSO}_4$ with $M_r = 120.4$. The ion of Z in $\text{ZSO}_4$ has 10 electrons. Element Z has three isotopes L, M and N with neutron numbers 12, 13 and 14 respectively. The percentage abundances of isotopes M and N are the same. What is the percentage abundance of isotope L?

($M_r$: $\text{SO}_4^{2-} = 96.1$)

Equal small masses of lithium, sodium and potassium are added separately to three beakers each containing 100 g of an alcohol. Each metal reacts similarly. The final mass of each beaker is compared to the initial mass before the metal was added. Which of the following statements is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: The beaker with lithium added would decrease in mass the most.

options.label: B
options.text: The beaker with sodium added would decrease in mass the most.

options.label: C
options.text: The beaker with potassium added would decrease in mass the most.

options.label: D
options.text: All three beakers would show the same decrease in mass.

options.label: E
options.text: The beaker with lithium added would increase in mass the most.

options.label: F
options.text: The beaker with sodium added would increase in mass the most.

options.label: G
options.text: The beaker with potassium added would increase in mass the most.

options.label: H
options.text: All three beakers would show the same increase in mass.

validation.answer_text: The beaker with potassium added would increase in mass the most.

prompt.stem:
Four experiments used different quantities of $2\text{ mol dm}^{-3}$ HCl and $2\text{ mol dm}^{-3}$ NaOH in insulated polystyrene cups. Maximum temperature was recorded and results plotted on a graph as shown. Two straight lines were drawn and extrapolated. What is the molar enthalpy change for the neutralisation reaction, in $\text{kJ mol}^{-1}$?

(specific heat capacity of solutions $= 4\text{ J g}^{-1}\text{ }^{\circ}\text{C}^{-1}$; density $= 1\text{ g cm}^{-3}$; all heat transferred to solution)

In an electrolysis of aluminium oxide using inert electrodes, the current flows at $5.00\times10^{-6}$ moles of electrons per second. Assume only aluminium oxide is present and the aluminium is the single isotope $^{27}_{13}$Al. What mass of aluminium is produced in 48 seconds?

X is a solution of sulfuric acid. $20.0\text{ cm}^3$ of X is diluted to produce $500\text{ cm}^3$ of solution Y. $10.0\text{ cm}^3$ of Y is exactly neutralised by $40.0\text{ cm}^3$ of $0.0500\text{ mol dm}^{-3}$ aqueous potassium hydroxide. What is the concentration of sulfuric acid in X?

Which of the following could lead to phenotypic variation between a father and his son?

1. time spent in sunlight
2. their genomes
3. their diets

Which of the following cells do not contain mitochondria?

1. bacterial cells
2. embryonic stem cells
3. mature red blood cells
4. potato cells

A section of double-stranded DNA contains 4500 base pairs. 10% of the bases present are adenine. Which of the following statements is/are correct?

1. There are 450 thymine bases present.
2. 40% of the bases present are cytosine.
3. There are 3600 guanine bases present.
The diagram shows part of the carbon cycle with boxes P, Q, R and S. Which row identifies the correct descriptions for boxes P, Q, R and S?

options.label: A
options.text: P: carbon-rich compounds in animals; Q: carbon-rich compounds in decomposers; R: CO2 in atmosphere; S: carbon-rich compounds in plants

options.label: B
options.text: P: carbon-rich compounds in decomposers; Q: CO2 in atmosphere; R: carbon-rich compounds in plants; S: carbon-rich compounds in animals

options.label: C
options.text: P: CO2 in atmosphere; Q: carbon-rich compounds in animals; R: carbon-rich compounds in decomposers; S: carbon-rich compounds in plants

options.label: D
options.text: P: carbon-rich compounds in animals; Q: CO2 in atmosphere; R: carbon-rich compounds in plants; S: carbon-rich compounds in decomposers

options.label: E
options.text: P: CO2 in atmosphere; Q: carbon-rich compounds in decomposers; R: carbon-rich compounds in plants; S: carbon-rich compounds in animals

options.label: F
options.text: P: carbon-rich compounds in decomposers; Q: CO2 in atmosphere; R: carbon-rich compounds in animals; S: carbon-rich compounds in plants

options.label: G
options.text: P: carbon-rich compounds in animals; Q: carbon-rich compounds in decomposers; R: CO2 in atmosphere; S: carbon-rich compounds in plants

options.label: H
options.text: P: carbon-rich compounds in decomposers; Q: carbon-rich compounds in animals; R: CO2 in atmosphere; S: carbon-rich compounds in plants

validation.answer_text: P: carbon-rich compounds in animals; Q: CO2 in atmosphere; R: carbon-rich compounds in plants; S: carbon-rich compounds in decomposers

prompt.stem: The diagram shows two human gametes, cell P and cell Q, fusing to form cell R. Cell P carries an additional copy of one of its chromosomes so that it has one more chromosome than cell Q. Cell R divides to form two cells S and T. S and T grow into two separate individuals. Which row shows the correct number of chromosomes in the nucleus of cell R and in the nucleus of cell T?

The graph shows how the activity of pepsin and trypsin varies with pH. In the human digestive system:

1. pepsin is most active in the stomach.
2. trypsin would be inactive in the stomach.
3. pepsin could be a substrate for trypsin.
4. pepsin is most active at low acidity and trypsin most active at high acidity.

Which statements are correct?

The table shows concentrations of substances in blood entering and leaving three different organs (kidney, small intestine, chambers of the right side of the heart) 10 minutes after eating a carbohydrate-rich meal.

Organ 1: glucose in=9.0, O2 in=65, urea in=3.0 mg dm-3; glucose out=9.0, O2 out=65, urea out=3.0
Organ 2: glucose in=9.0, O2 in=85, urea in=3.0; glucose out=9.0, O2 out=70, urea out=2.4
Organ 3: glucose in=9.0, O2 in=85, urea in=3.0; glucose out=12.0, O2 out=70, urea out=3.0

Which row identifies the organs?

options.label: A
options.text: organ 1: kidney; organ 2: small intestine; organ 3: right heart

options.label: B
options.text: organ 1: kidney; organ 2: right heart; organ 3: small intestine

options.label: C
options.text: organ 1: small intestine; organ 2: kidney; organ 3: right heart

options.label: D
options.text: organ 1: small intestine; organ 2: right heart; organ 3: kidney

options.label: E
options.text: organ 1: right heart; organ 2: small intestine; organ 3: kidney

options.label: F
options.text: organ 1: right heart; organ 2: kidney; organ 3: small intestine

validation.answer_text: organ 1: right heart; organ 2: kidney; organ 3: small intestine

prompt.stem:
In one type of SCID, white blood cells cannot make functional enzyme ADA, which is necessary for these cells to divide by mitosis during an immune response. A gene therapy treatment inserts a gene into the DNA of stem cells from the bone marrow of a person with this condition. These cells can then be returned to the body, divide and differentiate into white blood cells. Which of the following statements correctly describe(s) how this method of gene therapy might work?

1. Genetically altered stem cells differentiate into white blood cells that are able to produce functional ADA.
2. Genetically altered stem cells can differentiate into gametes so that offspring will not inherit SCID.
3. The sequence of the bases in the DNA of the white blood cells, derived from the genetically altered stem cells, has changed.

Which of the following could be a result of the addition of a large amount of organic material into a slow flowing river?

1. A decrease in biodiversity in the river.
2. A reduction in the oxygen concentration gradient between the air and the water.
3. An increase in the size of at least one aquatic population.

The table shows information about sickle cell anaemia and malaria:

| genotype | phenotype | comment |
|---|---|---|
| MM | does not show sickle cell anaemia | can be infected with malaria |
| Mm | does not show sickle cell anaemia | shows resistance to malaria |
| mm | shows sickle cell anaemia | shows more resistance to malaria than Mm |

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

1. In areas without malaria, human populations are likely to have a low number of people with the m allele.
2. In areas with malaria, only those individuals that are heterozygous will be able to pass on their alleles to the next generation.
3. Presence of malaria has caused a mutation of the M allele to the m allele leading to an increased chance of survival in the heterozygous state.

The table shows the concentration of potassium ions in several different locations:

| location | concentration |
|---|---|
| bacterial cell cytoplasm | 30 mmol dm-3 |
| mammalian blood plasma | 4000 umol dm-3 |
| mammalian heart cell cytoplasm | 1.0 x 10^2 mmol dm-3 |
| sea water | 3.0 x 10^4 umol dm-3 |
| yeast cell cytoplasm | 300 mmol dm-3 |

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

1. A mammalian heart cell needs energy from respiration in order to obtain more potassium ions from blood plasma.
2. If a yeast cell is placed in sea water then it will lose potassium ions by osmosis.
3. There is no concentration gradient for potassium ions between a bacterial cell and sea water.

The graph shows the mass of product produced over time for an enzyme-controlled reaction in two different conditions, X and Y. All other variables were kept constant. In condition X, the reaction produces more product overall and more quickly than in condition Y. Which of the following could be correct for this graph?

1. Condition X is a higher pH than condition Y.
2. Condition X has less substrate supplied than condition Y.
3. Condition X is a lower pH than condition Y.
4. Condition X has more substrate supplied than condition Y.

A student investigated the endothermic reaction of photosynthesis in pondweed. The apparatus is shown in the diagram. The experiment was left for 5 minutes and the distance moved by the gas bubble along a capillary tube of 2 mm diameter was recorded. Using these values, the rate of gas production is 2 mm³ per minute. Which row of the table is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: total distance: 0.5 mm; endothermic reason: releases energy; moved further away: gas bubble moves more slowly to the right

options.label: B
options.text: total distance: 0.5 mm; endothermic reason: takes in energy; moved further away: gas bubble moves more slowly to the left

options.label: C
options.text: total distance: 2.0 mm; endothermic reason: releases energy; moved further away: gas bubble moves more slowly to the left

options.label: D
options.text: total distance: 2.0 mm; endothermic reason: releases energy; moved further away: gas bubble moves more slowly to the right

options.label: E
options.text: total distance: 2.5 mm; endothermic reason: takes in energy; moved further away: gas bubble moves more slowly to the left

options.label: F
options.text: total distance: 2.5 mm; endothermic reason: takes in energy; moved further away: gas bubble moves more slowly to the right

options.label: G
options.text: total distance: 10.0 mm; endothermic reason: releases energy; moved further away: gas bubble moves more slowly to the left

options.label: H
options.text: total distance: 10.0 mm; endothermic reason: takes in energy; moved further away: gas bubble moves more slowly to the right

validation.answer_text: total distance: 10.0 mm; endothermic reason: takes in energy; moved further away: gas bubble moves more slowly to the right

prompt.stem:
The abundance of a plant species in a 100 m² area of grassland was measured using large quadrats (50 cm x 50 cm) and small quadrats (10 cm x 10 cm). 10 large quadrats and then 10 small quadrats were used to sample the area randomly. Which of the following statements is/are correct?

1. The overall density in the grassland calculated from sampling with either size quadrat will always be the same.
2. The overall density in the grassland calculated from sampling will always be 1 plant per m².
3. The frequency obtained using the small quadrat will always be lower than that obtained with the large quadrat.

The diagram shows changes in the volume of the ventricles and the ECG trace during two consecutive heart beats. Points X, Y and Z are labelled on the graph. Which row is correct about the events happening at X, Y and Z?

options.label: A
options.text: X: blood pumped into aorta; Y: AV valves open; Z: atria contracting

options.label: B
options.text: X: blood pumped into aorta; Y: AV valves closed; Z: atria contracting

options.label: C
options.text: X: blood pumped into atria; Y: AV valves open; Z: ventricles contracting

options.label: D
options.text: X: blood pumped into atria; Y: AV valves closed; Z: ventricles contracting

options.label: E
options.text: X: blood pumped into pulmonary artery; Y: AV valves open; Z: ventricles contracting

options.label: F
options.text: X: blood pumped into pulmonary artery; Y: AV valves closed; Z: ventricles contracting

options.label: G
options.text: X: blood pumped into pulmonary vein; Y: AV valves open; Z: atria contracting

options.label: H
options.text: X: blood pumped into pulmonary vein; Y: AV valves closed; Z: atria contracting

validation.answer_text: X: blood pumped into aorta; Y: AV valves open; Z: atria contracting

prompt.stem: The diagram represents the maternal and foetal blood flow between P and Q in a section of the placenta of a healthy small mammal. The concentration of oxygen in both maternal and foetal blood was measured at regular distances along this section. Which graph illustrates the concentration of oxygen in both maternal and foetal blood between P and Q?

Catalase is an enzyme found inside plant and animal cells. When catalase is added to hydrogen peroxide, bubbles of oxygen gas are formed. Red blood cells were placed into either water or plasma. Plant cells were placed into either water or 0.5 mol dm-3 sucrose solution. Both were placed in the dark. Assume that hydrogen peroxide and catalase do not cross the cell surface membrane. Which row shows the results when hydrogen peroxide was added? (tick = oxygen bubbles form; cross = oxygen bubbles will not form)

options.label: A
options.text: RBCs in plasma: tick; plant cells in sucrose: tick; RBCs in water: tick; plant cells in water: cross

options.label: B
options.text: RBCs in plasma: cross; plant cells in sucrose: cross; RBCs in water: cross; plant cells in water: tick

options.label: C
options.text: RBCs in plasma: cross; plant cells in sucrose: cross; RBCs in water: tick; plant cells in water: cross

options.label: D
options.text: RBCs in plasma: cross; plant cells in sucrose: tick; RBCs in water: tick; plant cells in water: tick

options.label: E
options.text: RBCs in plasma: tick; plant cells in sucrose: cross; RBCs in water: cross; plant cells in water: cross

options.label: F
options.text: RBCs in plasma: tick; plant cells in sucrose: tick; RBCs in water: tick; plant cells in water: tick

validation.answer_text: RBCs in plasma: cross; plant cells in sucrose: cross; RBCs in water: tick; plant cells in water: cross

prompt.stem:
Three different methods X, Y and Z of controlling pathogens are compared:

X: affects bacterial pathogens: yes; affects viral pathogens: yes; requires movement of human cells: yes; uses molecule released from human cells: no
Y: affects bacterial pathogens: yes; affects viral pathogens: no; requires movement of human cells: no; uses molecule released from human cells: no
Z: affects bacterial pathogens: yes; affects viral pathogens: yes; requires movement of human cells: no; uses molecule released from human cells: yes

Which row is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: X: may be stimulated by injections of inactivated pathogen; Y: taking an antibiotic; Z: may work by cells engulfing pathogens

options.label: B
options.text: X: may be stimulated by injections of inactivated pathogen; Y: may work by cells engulfing pathogens; Z: taking an antibiotic

options.label: C
options.text: X: taking an antibiotic; Y: may be stimulated by injections of inactivated pathogen; Z: may work by cells engulfing pathogens

options.label: D
options.text: X: taking an antibiotic; Y: may work by cells engulfing pathogens; Z: may be stimulated by injections of inactivated pathogen

options.label: E
options.text: X: may work by cells engulfing pathogens; Y: may be stimulated by injections of inactivated pathogen; Z: taking an antibiotic

options.label: F
options.text: X: may work by cells engulfing pathogens; Y: taking an antibiotic; Z: may be stimulated by injections of inactivated pathogen

validation.answer_text: X: may work by cells engulfing pathogens; Y: taking an antibiotic; Z: may be stimulated by injections of inactivated pathogen

prompt.stem: In rabbits, the dominant allele B gives black colour and the recessive allele b gives brown colour. Two male black rabbits of unknown genotype each mated with a different female brown rabbit. What is the expected proportion of brown offspring if both male rabbits are heterozygous, and what is the expected proportion of brown offspring if only one male rabbit is heterozygous?

The three family trees show the inheritance of three different genetic conditions, each controlled by one gene with one dominant and one recessive allele. In which of the family trees must the male parent be heterozygous?

(Assume that no new mutations occur and that the genes are not found on the X chromosome.)
